title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Edward recalls the day his grandfather died, describing the experience of being forced by his mother to hold his grandfather's hand one last time before leaving the room.

Later, at the Brawnshan Hotel, Polo and Edward enter a lavish restaurant where Luca Lazari approaches them to apologize for misinterpreting messages and spreading rumors. A short, stout man named Raphael Bork admits to being the server responsible for the victims. Bork explains that at 7:15 PM on Thursday, he delivered "dinner-time tea" to Room 317 for Harriet Sipper, Edith Gainsborough, and Richard Nigges. The order consisted of tea, various sandwiches, a mixed cake set, and scones with jam and cream. Bork confirms that he did not deliver any sherry to Mr. Nigges.

Edward notes a discrepancy: while the tea cups in Harriet Sipple's room were consistent with what Bork delivered, a sherry glass was found in Richard Nigges' room despite no staff member admitting to delivering sherry. Lazari confirms the glass is a hotel glass, which is highly unusual.

Poirot questions Bork further about the victims' behavior. Bork describes the trio as "sneaky" and laughing, noting they were gossiping about an old woman and a young man—specifically, a story about a young man leaving an old woman to run off with someone else. Poirot observes that the three victims were friends who were unaware of their impending deaths. As Poirot reflects on this, a pale, black-haired young man at a nearby table jumps up with a terrified expression, appearing eager to speak.
